name: Microsoft Corporation
description: Microsoft is a global technology leader providing software, services, devices, and solutions. Its business is centered on the Cloud, AI, and productivity tools, primarily through the Azure cloud platform and the Microsoft 365 suite.
businessModel: Microsoft generates revenue through Azure cloud services, software licensing (Windows, Office), hardware (Surface, Xbox), and increasingly through AI-integrated services via its partnership with OpenAI.
geopoliticalSummary: Microsoft faces significant risks from trade disputes, particularly with China, and varying international regulatory environments such as the GDPR and DMA in Europe. Its global footprint exposes it to cybersecurity threats from state-sponsored actors.

keySuppliers.name: NVIDIA
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Primary supplier of GPUs for Azure AI infrastructure.

keyCustomers.name: General Electric
keyCustomers.strategicNote: Major enterprise customer for Azure and AI services.

manufacturingLocations.note: Significant R&D and manufacturing presence in China for hardware.

commodityDependencies.note: Massive energy requirements for global datacenter operations.

regionalExposures.note: Strict compliance with GDPR and Digital Markets Act required.
Revenue from China is less than 5% but supply chain and R&D exposure is significant.

infrastructureDependencies.name: Trans-Pacific (North) Shipping Route
infrastructureDependencies.note: Used for hardware shipments from Asian suppliers.

aiComputeStack.what: Azure AI services and OpenAI partnership
aiComputeStack.note: Deep integration with enterprise workflows via Office 365.

hbmRelationship.note: Azure datacenters utilize high-bandwidth memory via GPU clusters.

customASICPartnerships.note: First custom AI accelerator designed for Azure.

keyAICustomers.name: OpenAI
keyAICustomers.note: Microsoft is the exclusive cloud provider for OpenAI.

vsNVIDIA.note: Microsoft is NVIDIA's largest customer while also developing competing custom silicon.

competitiveMoat: First-mover advantage in generative AI via OpenAI
Massive installed base of enterprise software
keyAIRisks: High capital expenditure for AI infrastructure
Regulatory scrutiny of AI safety and competition

sources.name: Microsoft 10-K FY2024 — Item 1A Risk Factors
sources.verbatimQuote: Our international operations are subject to many of the same risks as our domestic operations, as well as additional risks.

name: NVIDIA Corporation
description: NVIDIA is the leading designer of graphics processing units (GPUs) and AI computing systems. Its products power the majority of the world's AI training and inference workloads.
businessModel: Revenue is primarily from the sale of GPUs, networking components (Mellanox), and software for AI and graphics applications.
geopoliticalSummary: NVIDIA faces critical risks from US-China export controls and its near-total reliance on TSMC in Taiwan for manufacturing.

keySuppliers.name: TSMC
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Sole source for advanced AI GPUs.

commodityDependencies.note: Dependent on Taiwan for all high-end production.

regionalExposures.note: US government restrictions on high-end GPU exports to China.
Manufacturing concentration in Taiwan is an existential risk.

infrastructureDependencies.name: Trans-Pacific (North) Shipping Route
infrastructureDependencies.note: Critical for shipping GPUs from Taiwan to US datacenters.

aiComputeStack.what: H100, B100 GPUs and CUDA software platform
aiComputeStack.note: CUDA ecosystem creates massive software lock-in.

hbmRelationship.note: Major buyer of HBM3/HBM3E from SK Hynix and Micron.

keyAICustomers.name: Meta Platforms
keyAICustomers.note: Purchasing hundreds of thousands of H100s.

vsNVIDIA.note: Market leader setting the pace for the industry.

sources.name: NVIDIA 10-K FY2024 — Item 1A Risk Factors
sources.verbatimQuote: We are subject to risks associated with our international operations.

name: Alphabet Inc.
description: Alphabet is the parent company of Google, specialized in internet-related services and products, including search, advertising, cloud computing, and AI (DeepMind).
businessModel: Primary revenue comes from Google Search advertising, YouTube ads, and Google Cloud services. AI is integrated throughout all products.
geopoliticalSummary: Alphabet faces significant regulatory headwinds globally, including antitrust lawsuits in the US and EU, and complex data sovereignty laws in various regions.

keySuppliers.name: Broadcom
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Partner in designing TPU (Tensor Processing Unit) AI chips.

manufacturingLocations.note: Manufacturing of Pixel hardware and some server components.

commodityDependencies.note: Large-scale energy consumption for global datacenters.

regionalExposures.note: Ongoing antitrust and privacy compliance challenges.

infrastructureDependencies.name: Dunant Submarine Cable
infrastructureDependencies.note: Google-owned cable connecting US and France.

aiComputeStack.what: Gemini models, Vertex AI, and TPU infrastructure
aiComputeStack.note: Unique vertical integration from chips (TPU) to models (Gemini).

keyAICustomers.name: Anthropic
keyAICustomers.note: Anthropic uses Google Cloud and TPUs.

vsNVIDIA.note: Google Cloud offers NVIDIA GPUs while pushing its own TPUs as an alternative.

competitiveMoat: Massive data advantage from Search and YouTube
keyAIRisks: Disruption of search business by LLMs

sources.name: Alphabet 10-K FY2024 — Item 1A Risk Factors
sources.verbatimQuote: We are subject to risks associated with our international operations, which could harm our business.

name: Amazon.com Inc.
description: Amazon is an American multinational technology company focusing on e-commerce, cloud computing (AWS), online advertising, digital streaming, and AI.
businessModel: Revenue is diversified across online retail, third-party seller services, AWS cloud infrastructure, and advertising.
geopoliticalSummary: Amazon's global logistics network is sensitive to trade route disruptions and international trade policies. AWS faces data residency requirements in various jurisdictions.

keySuppliers.name: Various Shipping Lines
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Reliance on global shipping for retail inventory.

manufacturingLocations.note: Manufacturing of Kindle, Fire TV, and Echo devices.

commodityDependencies.note: Logistics network is highly sensitive to energy prices.

regionalExposures.note: Shipping disruptions impacting retail supply chain between Asia and Europe.

infrastructureDependencies.name: Suez Canal Shipping Route
infrastructureDependencies.note: Critical for retail goods from Asia to Europe.

aiComputeStack.what: AWS Bedrock, Trainium and Inferentia chips
aiComputeStack.note: Focusing on providing a broad range of models via Bedrock.

customASICPartnerships.note: Custom chip optimized for training deep learning models.

vsNVIDIA.note: AWS offers the widest selection of NVIDIA GPUs while promoting its own cheaper Trainium chips.

competitiveMoat: Deep integration with global logistics data
keyAIRisks: Slow start in generative AI compared to Microsoft
Data privacy concerns in retail AI

sources.name: Amazon 10-K FY2024 — Item 1A Risk Factors
sources.verbatimQuote: We are subject to general and specific risks of doing business internationally.

name: Meta Platforms Inc.
description: Meta Platforms builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. Its products include Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Reality Labs (VR/AR).
businessModel: Revenue is almost entirely from advertising across its family of apps.
geopoliticalSummary: Meta is heavily exposed to data privacy regulations (GDPR) and has been banned in Russia. Its reliance on China-based resellers for ad revenue is a significant risk.

revenueByGeography.note: Includes significant revenue from China-based resellers

keySuppliers.name: NVIDIA
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Major buyer of GPUs for Llama model training.

keyCustomers.name: E-commerce companies (China)
keyCustomers.strategicNote: Significant ad spend from Chinese companies like Temu and Shein.

regionalExposures.note: Ongoing legal battles over data transfers between EU and US.
High reliance on Chinese advertisers despite the app being blocked in China.

infrastructureDependencies.name: Amitie Submarine Cable
infrastructureDependencies.note: Connects US, UK, and France; Meta is a part-owner.

aiComputeStack.what: Llama series of large language models
aiComputeStack.note: Llama has become the industry standard for open-source AI.

vsNVIDIA.note: Meta is one of NVIDIA's largest customers, with Zuckerberg stating they will have 350,000 H100s by end of 2024.

competitiveMoat: Huge user base for AI deployment (Instagram/WhatsApp)
keyAIRisks: Open-source strategy may commoditize their own advantage
High CAPEX for AI infrastructure without immediate direct revenue

aiMarketShareSources.note: Llama is the most downloaded open-source LLM.

sources.name: Meta 10-K FY2024 — Item 1A Risk Factors
sources.verbatimQuote: Our business is subject to complex and evolving laws and regulations worldwide.

name: Equinix Inc.
description: Equinix is the world's digital infrastructure company, operating over 250 data centers across five continents. It provides colocation and interconnection services to enterprises and cloud providers.
businessModel: Revenue is generated through recurring monthly fees for colocation space, power, and interconnection services.
geopoliticalSummary: Equinix faces risks from rising energy costs globally, particularly in Europe, and the complexity of managing physical assets in 70+ markets.

keySuppliers.name: Local Power Utilities
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Electricity is the primary input cost.

keyCustomers.name: Microsoft/Google/Amazon
keyCustomers.strategicNote: Hyperscalers use Equinix for on-ramps and edge nodes.

commodityDependencies.note: Energy price volatility is a major risk factor.

regionalExposures.note: Energy grid stability and pricing in Europe are critical.

infrastructureDependencies.name: Central European Power Grid
infrastructureDependencies.note: Critical for operating major hubs in Frankfurt and Amsterdam.

aiComputeStack.what: IBX data centers and xScale hyperscale facilities
aiComputeStack.note: AI clusters require specific power and cooling density that Equinix provides.

keyAICustomers.name: NVIDIA
keyAICustomers.note: Partner for Equinix Private AI with NVIDIA DGX.

vsNVIDIA.note: Equinix hosts NVIDIA's managed AI infrastructure for enterprise customers.

competitiveMoat: xScale program for hyperscale AI demand
keyAIRisks: Power availability constraints in key markets
High cost of upgrading older centers for high-density AI

sources.name: Equinix 10-K FY2024 — Item 1A Risk Factors
sources.verbatimQuote: We are subject to risks associated with international operations.

name: Broadcom Inc.
description: Broadcom is a global technology leader that designs, develops, and supplies semiconductor and infrastructure software solutions. It is a key player in AI networking and custom ASIC design.
businessModel: Revenue is derived from semiconductor sales (networking, broadband, wireless) and infrastructure software (VMware).
geopoliticalSummary: Broadcom has significant revenue exposure to China and relies heavily on TSMC in Taiwan for manufacturing. The VMware acquisition has increased its software concentration.

revenueByGeography.note: Includes shipments to China-based ODMs and direct sales

keySuppliers.name: TSMC
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Sole source for advanced node networking and AI chips.

keyCustomers.name: Google
keyCustomers.strategicNote: Major customer for custom AI accelerators (TPUs).

commodityDependencies.note: Dependent on Taiwan for advanced nodes.

regionalExposures.note: Heavy reliance on China for product shipments.

infrastructureDependencies.name: Trans-Pacific (North) Shipping Route
infrastructureDependencies.note: Chip shipments from Taiwan to US/Global markets.

aiComputeStack.what: Custom AI ASICs (TPUs) and Tomahawk switches
aiComputeStack.note: Monopoly-like position in high-end AI networking switches.

hbmRelationship.note: Custom ASICs integrate HBM from third parties.

customASICPartnerships.note: Long-term partnership with Google on TPU development.

vsNVIDIA.note: Competes with NVIDIA's Mellanox networking and provides an alternative to NVIDIA GPUs via custom ASICs.

competitiveMoat: Broad IP portfolio in high-speed SerDes
keyAIRisks: NVIDIA's InfiniBand competing with Ethernet in AI clusters
Export controls on high-end networking chips to China

sources.name: Broadcom 10-K FY2024 — Item 1A Risk Factors
sources.verbatimQuote: We are subject to risks associated with our international operations and global supply chain.

name: Advanced Micro Devices Inc.
description: AMD is a global semiconductor company that designs and develops computer processors and related technologies. Its MI300 series GPUs are the primary competitors to NVIDIA in the AI market.
businessModel: Revenue is derived from Data Center (EPYC, Instinct), Client (Ryzen), Gaming (Radeon), and Embedded (Xilinx) segments.
geopoliticalSummary: AMD relies heavily on TSMC in Taiwan for manufacturing and faces significant export control risks related to the China market.

keySuppliers.name: TSMC
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Sole source for all advanced 5nm and 3nm products.

keyCustomers.name: Microsoft
keyCustomers.strategicNote: Major buyer of Instinct MI300X GPUs for Azure.

regionalExposures.note: Export controls prevent sale of highest-end AI chips to China.

infrastructureDependencies.name: Trans-Pacific (North) Shipping Route
infrastructureDependencies.note: Shipments from Taiwan to US/Global markets.

aiComputeStack.what: Instinct MI300 series and ROCm software
aiComputeStack.note: Primary alternative to NVIDIA H100 in the merchant GPU market.

hbmRelationship.note: Instinct MI300 uses more HBM than NVIDIA H100.

vsNVIDIA.note: Aggressively competing on price-performance and memory capacity with MI300X.

competitiveMoat: Leadership in x86 CPUs for AI servers
keyAIRisks: Execution risk in catching up to Blackwell

aiMarketShareSources.note: Gaining share from a low base.

sources.name: AMD 10-K FY2024 — Item 1A Risk Factors
sources.verbatimQuote: We are subject to risks associated with our international operations.

name: Super Micro Computer Inc.
description: Supermicro is a global leader in high-performance, high-efficiency server technology and innovation. It provides end-to-end green computing solutions for Enterprise, Cloud, AI, and Edge markets.
businessModel: Revenue is generated from the sale of server and storage systems, subsystems, and accessories, with a focus on AI-optimized rack solutions.
geopoliticalSummary: Supermicro has manufacturing in Taiwan and Malaysia to mitigate China risks but faces intense regulatory scrutiny regarding its export compliance and accounting.

keySuppliers.name: NVIDIA
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Highly dependent on NVIDIA GPU allocations for its server clusters.

keyCustomers.name: CoreWeave
keyCustomers.strategicNote: Major AI cloud provider using SMCI racks.

regionalExposures.note: Investigation into potential export control violations regarding China.

infrastructureDependencies.name: Trans-Pacific (North) Shipping Route
infrastructureDependencies.note: Supply chain from Taiwan to US assembly plants.

aiComputeStack.note: Differentiated by 'Building Block' architecture and liquid cooling.

keyAICustomers.name: Tesla
keyAICustomers.note: Supplying server racks for Tesla's Dojo and AI clusters.

vsNVIDIA.note: Close relationship allows for 'first-to-market' status with new NVIDIA architectures.

competitiveMoat: Speed to market for new CPU/GPU architectures
Expertise in liquid cooling for dense AI racks
keyAIRisks: Intense competition from Dell and HPE

aiMarketShareSources.note: Rapidly growing share in AI infrastructure.

sources.name: Supermicro 10-K FY2024 — Item 1A Risk Factors
sources.verbatimQuote: Our international operations are subject to risks that could harm our business.

name: Arista Networks Inc.
description: Arista Networks provides cloud networking solutions for large data center and campus environments. Its EOS software-defined networking is a key enabler for hyperscale AI clusters.
businessModel: Revenue is derived from the sale of networking switches and routers, and related software and support services.
geopoliticalSummary: Arista has significant supply chain exposure to Southeast Asia and faces risks from trade policies affecting high-end networking equipment.

keySuppliers.name: Broadcom
keySuppliers.strategicNote: Arista switches are built almost exclusively on Broadcom's merchant silicon.

keyCustomers.name: Microsoft
keyCustomers.strategicNote: Major customer for data center switching.

keyCustomers.name: Meta Platforms
keyCustomers.strategicNote: Major customer for AI backbone networking.

manufacturingLocations.note: Primary manufacturing hub for global shipments.

commodityDependencies.note: Dependent on merchant silicon (Broadcom) for switch fabrication.

regionalExposures.note: Concentration of contract manufacturing in Malaysia.

infrastructureDependencies.name: Trans-Pacific (South) Shipping Route
infrastructureDependencies.note: Supply chain from SE Asia to North American customers.

aiComputeStack.note: Arista is the leader in high-speed Ethernet for AI, competing with InfiniBand.

keyAICustomers.name: Meta Platforms
keyAICustomers.note: Arista switches power Meta's AI back-end clusters.

vsNVIDIA.note: Arista's Ethernet solutions compete directly with NVIDIA's Mellanox InfiniBand for AI cluster interconnects.

competitiveMoat: Leadership in the shift from InfiniBand to Ethernet for AI
keyAIRisks: Supply chain disruptions in SE Asia

aiMarketShareSources.metric: High-End Data Center Switching Market Share

sources.name: Arista Networks 10-K FY2024 — Item 1A Risk Factors
sources.verbatimQuote: We are subject to risks associated with our international operations and global supply chain.
